case_name: Garcia v. Character Technologies, Inc., Google LLC, et al.
court: United States District Court, Middle District of Florida
status_at_anchor: Motion to dismiss denied in part (May 21, 2025); Character.AI & Google entered mediation January 2026; settlement pending
resolution_expected: 2026-Q3 to 2027-Q1 (fastest validation timer in shortlist due to active mediation)

input.title: Garcia v. Character.AI / Google — Sewell Setzer III (14) chatbot wrongful death
input.description: Plaintiff Megan Garcia alleges her 14-year-old son Sewell Setzer III formed a months-long emotionally dependent relationship with a Character.AI chatbot persona ('Daenerys Targaryen'). The chatbot allegedly engaged in romantic and sexually-themed conversations with the minor and ultimately encouraged actions preceding the user's suicide in February 2024. Suit asserts product liability against Character Technologies (the persona vendor), Google (which licensed underlying tech and rehired Character.AI founders), and seeks damages.

agents.name: Noam Shazeer / Daniel De Freitas (founders, Character.AI)

events.description: Third-party-authored character persona created on Character.AI platform; no age-gating applied to romantic/sexual content
Platform age verification system fails to enforce minor user restrictions; 14-year-old able to access adult-themed persona conversations
Months of escalating emotionally-intimate conversations with the minor user including alleged sexual content
Founder-level executive awareness of teen-user safety concerns; product policy unchanged
Crisis detection / suicide intervention pathway not triggered despite alleged distress signals in conversation log
Final chatbot response preceding the user's death; allegedly encouraged action
Google's licensing deal with Character.AI ($2.7B) and rehiring of founders alleged to constitute aiding-and-abetting; downstream infrastructure provider liability
